spec_match.rationale: The page successfully demonstrates modern CSS conditional rules including the new named-feature query by running live CSS.supports() checks.

interactive.rationale: Features are evaluated live on load via synchronous CSS.supports probes, visually reflecting local support for Grid, selector, at-rule, and named-feature.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The index description matches the interactive query explorer perfectly, giving a breakdown of all four condition types.

edge_cases.rationale: Only demonstrates supported conditions in modern Chrome; does not demonstrate the fallback behavior for unknown or invalid named-feature names, which should return false.

references.rationale: Explicitly links to the ChromeStatus entry in the 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared design variables and maintains clear WCAG AA color contrast (green/red color states over ivory paper backgrounds).

openQuestions.title: Test unknown and invalid named-feature behavior
openQuestions.detail: The explorer evaluates a valid Chrome 150 named-feature and Grid/selector support, but doesn't show what happens with unknown feature names. Add a card testing a dummy name like named-feature("invalid-feature-name") to verify the browser correctly returns false for unregistered feature names.
